task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a status label named 'Hardware Recall' with the type 'Undeployable'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Snipe-IT 'Status Labels' page showing a new entry named 'Hardware Recall' with its type explicitly listed as 'Undeployable'.

task_completion.description: Verify that all currently deployed 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' laptops have been checked in.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Snipe-IT 'All Assets' or 'Deployed Assets' list showing that no 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' laptops have a user assigned in the 'Checked Out To' or 'Deployed' column, or screenshots showing the check-in process being completed for these devices.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Hardware Recall' status label is assigned to all 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' assets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Snipe-IT 'All Assets' list filtered by 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' showing the 'Status' column updated to 'Hardware Recall' for these laptops.

task_completion.description: Verify that the official recall notice PDF is attached to the 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' asset model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Snipe-IT 'Asset Models' page for 'HP EliteBook 840 G7' showing 'CPSC_Recall_Notice.pdf' listed in the uploaded files or attachments section at the bottom of the model view.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'HP EliteBook 840 G8' laptops remain unmodified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Snipe-IT 'All Assets' list showing 'HP EliteBook 840 G8' assets retaining their original statuses (e.g., Deployed, Ready to Deploy) and NOT marked with the 'Hardware Recall' status.

integrity.description: The agent used the Snipe-IT web GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Snipe-IT interface, clicking buttons, and filling out web forms, rather than using terminal commands (like curl or SQL) to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: The agent uploaded the PDF file using the web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OS file picker dialog selecting 'CPSC_Recall_Notice.pdf' or the browser UI confirming the file upload, rather than terminal commands moving files into backend directories.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the output by typing the expected results into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The primary interactions are within the Snipe-IT web application, not a text editor, notes app, or HTML inspector used to alter the page visually.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Model to quarantine and update: 'HP EliteBook 840 G7'. Control Model to ignore: 'HP EliteBook 840 G8'. Expected Status Label Name: 'Hardware Recall' (must be type 'Undeployable'). The recall notice to upload is 'CPSC_Recall_Notice.pdf'.
